body:
**Status:** Draft. Do not merge until WebGPT accepts the R3 evidence bundle.

## Scope

First landing of `/review-extraction` in `agent-skills/main`. Adds:

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `skills/review-extraction/SKILL.md` | v2 schema + contracts (Collaboration model, 7-step protocol, Review output format with 9-item Complete plan, Complete plan requirements, Iteration stop conditions, blocked_on payload, Loop convergence, Iterative extraction-fix loop, Closure rule, Hard rules, Relationships to /review-code and best-practices) |
| `skills/review-extraction/scripts/build_golden_slice_bundle.py` | Deterministic bundle runner: page-only release-mode extraction, evidence rendering, precedence matcher, 4-bucket comparison, manifest v2 with 4 split mutation flags, optional_code/, blocked_on validator |
| `skills/review-extraction/README.md` | One-line pointer (from upstream zip) |
| `skills/review-extraction/UPSTREAM_MANIFEST.json` | Provenance (renamed from MANIFEST.json to avoid colliding with per-bundle manifests) |

## What's NOT in this PR

- No pdf_oxide / extractor changes
- No NIST preset / ledger changes
- No closure-matrix changes
- No LLM call
- No batch run

## Tests

Pinned in companion PR **grahama1970/pdf_oxide#1** (`pdf-oxide/gs001-r3-runner-tests`) — 17 cases covering manifest split flags, review_bundle wording, best_match_id guard, ambiguous_multiple not counted as matched, text_hint narrowing, blocked_on validator (5 cases), markdown rendering, manifest carry, §0 prepend. Tests live in pdf_oxide because that's where pytest runs alongside the rest of the PDF Lab suite.

## Evidence

R3.1 ZIP bundle for Golden Slice 001 (NIST page 28):
`/tmp/pdf-lab-golden-slices/nist_page_28_printed_page_1.zip` (1.3 MB)

- 9 expected rows → 6 matched / 0 ambiguous / 3 missing / 13 extras
- 3 missing rows flagged in `blocked_on.json` awaiting owner-routing decision

To be attached to WebGPT tab 837342436 via `surf webgpt.submit --no-activate --attach-file` once the §10 trigger fires (explicit "send to web gpt" OR stop-condition trigger (b)).

## Lifecycle

Disposable per the per-round branch hygiene rule. After WebGPT accepts code + evidence: squash-merge or delete. If rejected / superseded: close + delete this branch.

## Worktree note

This branch was created via `git worktree` from `main` so the active feature branches in this clone (`feat/webgpt-no-activate`, `battle/...`, `code-runner/...`, etc.) were not disturbed. The worktree at `/tmp/agent-skills-r3-worktree/` will be removed once the PR is opened.

commits.messageBody:
First landing of /review-extraction in agent-skills/main. Skill is the
contract + runner for golden-slice-style PDF extraction evidence
bundles (used by the PDF Lab / pdf_oxide loop).

SKILL.md highlights (v2 schema, R3 contract):

- Required bundle layout: manifest.json, review_bundle.md,
  command_log.txt, images/ (page_clean, pdf_oxide_overlay, optional
  human_labeled_page, element_crops), json/ (page_identity,
  expected_elements, pdf_oxide_raw_page, pdf_oxide_release_page,
  golden_slice_comparison, mismatches_by_owner, optional blocked_on),
  tables/, optional_code/ (when tooling code changed), optional
  blocked_on.md.
- Required manifest fields v2: 4 split mutation flags
  (extraction_code_mutated, preset_or_ledger_mutated, matrix_mutated,
  review_tooling_code_mutated); deprecated single
  code_or_preset_mutated MUST NOT be emitted.
- Required expected-elements shape v2: optional text_hint, bbox_hint,
  allowed_types, match_strategy.
- Required comparison table v2: 4-bucket classification (matched /
  ambiguous_multiple / missing / extra_extractor_element).
  best_match_id only when match_status == matched.
  reviewer_action_required true for ambiguous, missing, extra.
- Matcher precedence: text_hint -> bbox_hint(IoU >= 0.5) ->
  allowed_types -> family/type-synonym fallback. Strict criteria are
  hard filters (no silent fallback to ambiguous_multiple).
- Collaboration model: human labels page, WebGPT reasons + plans,
  project agent implements + reruns + resubmits, deterministic gates
  decide closure.
- Golden-slice 7-step review protocol.
- Review output format requires a 9-item Complete plan: scope /
  owner route / files / steps / rerun cmd / ZIP contents / tests /
  stop conditions / what not to mutate.
- Iteration stop conditions (6 cases): expected contract ambiguous,
  product judgment required, mismatch persists after two rounds,
  scope would broaden, core-vs-preset ownership lacks deterministic
  reproducer, cannot produce ZIP.
- Iterative extraction-fix loop: each resubmission MUST include
  updated release-mode JSON, overlay, crops, comparison, review
  bundle, and code diff/test output when code/preset/ledger/
  validator/runner changed.
- Closure rule: human annotation + WebGPT agreement = expected
  contract; project agent = executor; new ZIP each round = evidence;
  deterministic JSON match = closure.
- blocked_on payload contract: required when bundle ships under a
  stop-condition trigger. 6 required fields (schema_version,
  stop_condition_id, where_blocked, specific_question,
  artifacts_to_consult, what_i_will_NOT_do_without_decision,
  what_decision_unblocks). Runner validates at write time.
  Hard rule: no blocked_on -> no auto-send.

Runner (scripts/build_golden_slice_bundle.py) implements the full
contract: page-only release-mode extraction, evidence rendering,
matcher with precedence, 4-bucket comparison, manifest v2 with split
flags, optional_code/ generation, blocked_on validation + artifacts.

Companion PR in grahama1970/pdf_oxide adds 17 tests pinning the runner
contract: pdf-oxide/gs001-r3-runner-tests (PR #1).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.messageHeadline: skills: add /review-extraction with deterministic GS001 R3 runner

title: Add /review-extraction skill + GS001 R3 runner
